title: Airella praecox
intro: Airella praecox, also known as sea sandwort, is a type of flowering plant belonging to the grass family (Poaceae) that is native to coastal areas in Europe and North Africa. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health issues, including respiratory problems, digestive issues, and skin conditions, due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Airella praecox include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and other secondary metabolites that contribute to its medicinal properties. Airella praecox is often used in herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, as well as in the form of dried leaves or roots, to take advantage of its health benefits.
